title: Go Comments
content:
### Comments in Go

Comments are non-executable text that explain code.

### Single-Line Comments

Use `//` for single-line comments:

```go
// This is a single-line comment
x := 42 // inline comment
```

### Multi-Line Comments

Use `/* */` for block comments:

```go
/*
This is a multi-line comment.
It can span multiple lines.
*/
```

### Doc Comments

Every exported declaration should have a doc comment. Doc comments appear immediately before the declaration:

```go
// Package math provides basic mathematical constants and functions.
package math

// Pi is the mathematical constant pi.
const Pi = 3.14159

// Add returns the sum of two integers.
func Add(a, b int) int {
    return a + b
}
```

### Godoc Format

Doc comments are processed by `go doc` and rendered on [pkg.go.dev](https://pkg.go.dev).

| Comment Style | Usage |
|---------------|-------|
| `//` | Preferred for most comments |
| `// Package xxx` | Package-level doc comment |
| `/* */` | Block comments, disabled code |
| `//go:directive` | Compiler directives (special) |

> **Key Point:** Good comments explain *why*, not *what*. The code itself should make the *what* clear.

quiz.question: Which comment style is preferred for most Go comments?
What should doc comments explain?
options: What the code does and why
